message:
🌱 Add traefik: Support for private plugins mission (#1632)

Signed-off-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,80 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "traefik-7088-support-for-private-plugins",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "traefik: Support for private plugins",
+    "description": "Support for private plugins. Requested by 51+ users.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current traefik deployment",
+        "description": "Verify your traefik version and configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n traefik -l app.kubernetes.io/name=traefik\nhelm list -n traefik 2>/dev/null || echo \"Not installed via Helm\"\n```\nThis feature requires a working traefik installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review traefik configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant traefik configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get all -n traefik -l app.kubernetes.io/name=traefik\nkubectl get configmap -n traefik -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=traefik\n```\n### Do you want to request a *feature* or report a *bug*?\n\nFeature\n\n### What did you expect to see?\n\nSupport for private plugins. Connecting plugins from private repositories."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Support for private plugins",
+        "description": "Hey all ,\n\nIm sorry for the long time without another response from our side on that quite important topic here.\n \nBe assured, it's certainly not about \"letting things rot\" but rather, try to come up with a solution :)\nFirst of all, our goal has always been to commit to the Open Source philosophy\n```yaml\n- Path(/foo) && MyCustomRule(true) -> backend1.example.com\n- Path(/foo) && MyCustomRule(false) -> backend1.example.com\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Upgrade traefik to include the fix",
+        "description": "If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade traefik:\n```bash\nhelm repo update\nhelm upgrade traefik traefik/traefik --namespace traefik\n```\nVerify the upgrade:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n traefik\nhelm list -n traefik\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n traefik -l app.kubernetes.io/name=traefik\nkubectl get events -n traefik --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm the feature described in \"Support for private plugins\" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "Hey all ,\n\nIm sorry for the long time without another response from our side on that quite important topic here.\n \nBe assured, it's certainly not about \"letting things rot\" but rather, try to come up with a solution :)\nFirst of all, our goal has always been to commit to the Open Source philosophy and thus, Traefik plugins should be open sourced as well.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "- Path(/foo) && MyCustomRule(true) -> backend1.example.com\n- Path(/foo) && MyCustomRule(false) -> backend1.example.com",
+        "complexRule := Query(`foo=bar`) && !Query(`other=value`)\nreturn complexRule == input",
+        "/path/to/plugins\n               |- plugin A\n               |- plugin B\n               |- Plugin N"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "traefik",
+      "community",
+      "ingress",
+      "feature"
+    ],
+    "cncfProjects": [
+      "traefik"
+    ],
+    "targetResourceKinds": [],
+    "difficulty": "intermediate",
+    "issueTypes": [
+      "feature"
+    ],
+    "maturity": "community",
+    "sourceUrls": {
+      "issue": "https://github.com/traefik/traefik/issues/7088",
+      "repo": "https://github.com/traefik/traefik"
+    },
+    "reactions": 51,
+    "comments": 21,
+    "synthesizedBy": "copilot"
+  },
+  "prerequisites": {
+    "kubernetes": ">=1.24",
+    "tools": [
+      "kubectl"
+    ],
+    "description": "A running Kubernetes cluster with traefik installed or the issue environment reproducible."
+  },
